text: The Feeding of the Four Thousand

segments.text: In those days the crowd once again became very large, and they had nothing to eat. Jesus called the disciples to Him and said,
I have compassion for this crowd, because they have already been with Me three days and have nothing to eat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If I send them home hungry, they will faint along the way. For some of them have come a great distance.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His disciples replied,
Where in this desolate place could anyone find enough bread to feed all these people?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: How many loaves do you have?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked.
Seven,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they replied.

And He instructed the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then He took the seven loaves, gave thanks and broke them, and gave them to His disciples to set before the people. And they distributed them to the crowd.
They also had a few small fish, and Jesus blessed them and ordered that these be set before them as well.
The people ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

And about four thousand men[^48] were present.
As soon as Jesus had dismissed the crowd,
He got into the boat with His disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
Then the Pharisees came and began to argue with Jesus, testing Him by demanding from Him a sign from heaven.
Jesus sighed deeply in His spirit and said,
Why does this generation demand a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And He left them, got back into the boat, and crossed to the other side.

text: The Leaven of the Pharisees and of Herod

segments.text: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, except for one loaf they had with them in the boat.
Watch out!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He cautioned them.
Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of Herod.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they began to discuss with one another the fact that they had no bread.
Aware of their conversation, Jesus asked them,
Why are you debating about having no bread? Do you still not see or understand? Do you have such hard hearts?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Having eyes, do you not see? And having ears, do you not hear?’[^49] And do you not remember?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Twelve,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
And when I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Seven,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said.
Then He asked them,
Do you still not understand?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When they arrived at Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man and begged Jesus to touch him.
So He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village. Then He spit on the man’s eyes and placed His hands on him.
Can you see anything?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked.
The man looked up and said,
I can see the people, but they look like trees walking around.
segments.speaker: The man looked up and

segments.text: Once again Jesus placed His hands on the man’s eyes, and when he opened them his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
Jesus sent him home and said,
Do not go back into the village.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus and His disciples went on to the villages around Caesarea Philippi. On the way, He questioned His disciples:
Who do people say I am?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They replied,
Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But what about you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked.
Who do you say I am?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter answered,
You are the Christ.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about Him.
Then He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and that He must be killed and after three days rise again.
He spoke this message quite frankly, and Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him.
But Jesus, turning and looking at His disciples, rebuked Peter and said,
Get behind Me, Satan! For you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus called the crowd to Him along with His disciples, and He told them,
If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and for the gospel will save it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world, yet forfeit his soul?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone is ashamed of Me and My words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man will also be ashamed of him when He comes in His Father’s glory with the holy angels.
segments.speaker: Jesus
